name: Clavijo Castle Loop from Villamediana de Iregua
name_original: Subida al Castillo de Clavijo — vuelta desde Villamediana de Iregua
description: This moderate 27.6-mile (44.4 km) road cycling route climbs to historic Castillo de Clavijo, offering scenic views of La Rioja's vineyards.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Villamediana De Iregua are characterized by diverse terrain, ranging from riverside paths along the Iregua River to routes through extensive vineyards and challenging climbs into surrounding hills and valleys. The region offers varied landscapes, including the flat plains of "La Plana" and the elevated viewpoints of the Cerros de San Cristóbal and Santa María. Cyclists can explore historical sites and natural features, with routes often traversing agricultural areas and offering panoramic views of the Bajo Iregua. Best road cycling routes around Villamediana De Iregua The most popular road cycling route is Logroño: Viana, Moreda de Álava, and Oyón Loop, a 21.6 miles (34.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 34 minutes to complete. This easy route offers a scenic loop through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Clavijo Castle Loop from Villamediana de Iregua, a moderate 27.6 miles (44.4 km) path. This route starts from Villamediana de Iregua and includes a significant climb towards Clavijo Castle. Local road cyclists also love the Río Iregua – Alberite Fountain loop from Logroño, a 28.1 miles (45.2 km) trail leading through the Iregua River valley, often completed in about 5 hours 4 minutes. Road cycling around Villamediana De Iregua is defined by the Iregua River, extensive vineyards, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Villamediana De Iregua are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 7000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villamediana De Iregua's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Villamediana De Iregua?
A: There are over 170 road cycling routes documented around Villamediana De Iregua, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What are the general difficulty levels of road cycling routes in Villamediana De Iregua?
A: The routes cater to various abilities. You'll find approximately 10 easy routes, around 90 moderate routes, and about 70 difficult routes, ensuring options for beginners to experienced cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Villamediana De Iregua?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, from gentle riverside paths along the Iregua River to routes through extensive vineyards, such as those in 'La Plana' and the 'Zona de las Bodegas'. You can also expect more challenging climbs into surrounding hills and valleys, including elevated viewpoints like the Cerros de San Cristóbal and Santa María.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, many routes offer panoramic views. You can enjoy vistas of the Bajo Iregua and Logroño from areas like 'La Plana' and the Cerros de San Cristóbal and Santa María. The region also features a dedicated Mirador (viewpoint). For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31483658" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Trevijano – Soto en Cameros loop from Logroño&lt;/a&gt; offers stunning scenery.

Q: What natural features can I see while cycling in Villamediana De Iregua?
A: Cyclists can enjoy the tranquil banks of the Iregua River, vast vineyards that define the landscape, and the peaceful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/682737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Grajera Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; on the outskirts of Logroño. The nearby Parque Natural Sierra de Cebollera also offers lush forests and waterfalls for those willing to venture a bit further.

Q: Are there historical sites to explore on road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The ancient Vía Romana del Iregua provides a historical path for cycling. Routes may also pass by sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2706737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Clavijo Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4020897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Logroño Stone Bridge&lt;/a&gt; (part of the Camino Frances), and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4204668" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Co-Cathedral of Santa María de la Redonda&lt;/a&gt; in Logroño. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990743575/clavijo-castle-loop-from-villamediana-de-iregua" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Clavijo Castle Loop from Villamediana de Iregua&lt;/a&gt; is a great option for combining cycling with history.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Villamediana De Iregua?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 7,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villamediana De Iregua's varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and well-maintained routes.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990957699/logrono-viana-moreda-de-alava-and-oyon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Logroño: Viana, Moreda de Álava, and Oyón Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 34.7 km circular route. Another popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38216350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Iregua – Alberite Fountain loop from Logroño&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the Iregua River valley.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through vineyards?
A: Absolutely. The region is famous for its extensive vineyards, particularly in areas like 'La Plana' and the 'Zona de las Bodegas'. Many routes traverse these picturesque landscapes, offering beautiful views. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31916888" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain in Navarrete – Vineyards of La Rioja Alta loop from Lardero&lt;/a&gt; is a great example.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Villamediana De Iregua?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the diverse landscapes and generally mild climate of La Rioja suggest that spring and autumn would be ideal for road cycling, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, especially during harvest season in the vineyards.

Q: Are there options for longer road cycling tours?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate in length, there are longer options for those seeking more challenging rides. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31483658" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Trevijano – Soto en Cameros loop from Logroño&lt;/a&gt; covers over 60 km with significant elevation gain, providing a substantial challenge.

Q: What kind of cultural experiences can I combine with road cycling?
A: Beyond historical sites, you can explore the unique Barrio de las Bodegas, a district featuring numerous wineries where you can learn about Rioja wine production and enjoy tastings. The proximity to Logroño also allows for visits to its cultural and historical sites.
tips: It is an exceptional place to enjoy the views of the castle, the Basilica of Santiago and the Leza valley.

The town of Clavijo is located in the heart of Rioja's Tierra de Cameros, in a very broken terrain, surrounded by mountains with forests of holm oaks and oaks, Clavijo emerges, a place with a lot of legend and history. Barely 16 kilometers from Logroño, its fortress watches over from its battlements the roads that connect Castilla, Navarra and Aragón, the Leza river gorge and the Iregua and Ebro river valleys.
The Pichilín path is a paved road that runs parallel to the Leza riverbed. It is a very quiet road as it is very little frequented by motor vehicles.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in Villamediana de Iregua, which serves as the starting and return point for this route. Parking near the town center or residential areas is usually available. For the Castillo de Clavijo itself, parking options might be more limited due to its elevated and historic nature, so it's best to park in Villamediana de Iregua and cycle to the castle.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling. While dogs are generally welcome in public spaces in Spain, bringing a dog along on a racebike route of this difficulty and distance is not recommended for their safety or comfort. It's best to leave your furry friends at home for this particular ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The La Rioja region, where this route is located, generally offers pleasant cycling conditions in the spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the scenery, especially the vineyards, is particularly beautiful. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder temperatures and potential for adverse weather on the climbs.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and paths. Access to the Castillo de Clavijo itself may have specific visiting hours or fees if you wish to enter, but cycling to its base is free.

faqs.question: How challenging is the climb to Castillo de Clavijo?
faqs.answer: The ascent to Castillo de Clavijo is described as a "demanding end" and a "full-fledged Mountain pass." It involves significant gradients and requires good fitness. While the overall route is rated as moderate, this specific climb is the most strenuous part and will test your climbing abilities.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on this ride?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque scenery characteristic of the La Rioja region. The route often passes through beautiful vineyards and offers views of the Leza-Jubera valley and the town of Murillo de Río Leza. The majestic Sierra de Cameros mountain range provides a stunning backdrop, with panoramic vistas of broken terrain and forests of holm oaks and oaks.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The most significant point of interest is the historic Castillo de Clavijo, perched atop an imposing rock. You'll also pass by the Clavijo Viewpoint, offering great photo opportunities. The route also includes sections of the Camino de Pichilín and may feature historic elements like Roman roads and bridges over the Iregua River.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain for the majority of this route is paved, making it suitable for road bikes. However, be prepared for significant elevation gain, particularly on the demanding ascent to Castillo de Clavijo, which features steep gradients.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Villamediana de Iregua and Clavijo (especially near the castle area) may offer cafes, pubs, or restaurants where you can refuel. Villamediana de Iregua, being a larger town, will likely have more options for pre-ride coffee or post-ride meals.

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for this ride?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and significant climb, essential gear includes a well-maintained road bike, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, water bottles (at least two), energy snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), and a phone for navigation or emergencies. Sunscreen and sunglasses are also recommended, especially during sunnier months.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several local paths. You'll find sections that overlap with official trails such as [CIMA LR08] Clavijo (Ermita de Santiago) * Alberite, [CIMA LR08] Clavijo (Ermita de Santiago) * Albelda ~ LR-255 (Km. 7,8), and the Via Romana del Iregua, among others. These intersections offer potential for extending or modifying your ride if you wish to explore further.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: While the overall route is rated as moderate, the significant climb to Castillo de Clavijo makes it less ideal for absolute beginners. It requires a good level of fitness and some experience with sustained ascents. Beginners might find it challenging, but those with some road cycling experience looking for a rewarding climb could enjoy it.
